data.description:
## Problem
The existing epic decomposition builds lizard and jscpd adapters, but only the scc LOC payload is consumed by registered code_health metrics. Without an explicit integration slice, the epic can close with working adapters that users never observe.

## Plan
- After the adapter and LOC-migration stories land, add a code-health composition seam that invokes each configured signal producer at most once per metrics evaluation context and preserves honest Unavailable outcomes.
- Register structural/high-confidence code_health MetricSpecs for observable complexity and duplication summaries, alongside the scc-derived size metrics from 0734.
- Keep scc and jscpd as external PATH tools and lizard behind the metrics extra; no core hard dependency or import-time tool loading.
- Extend the metrics command/E2E output so Python and Go fixture projects expose loc, complexity, and duplication values when tools are present, and clear Unavailable entries when any producer is absent.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The code_health lens exposes registered complexity and duplication metrics backed by the lizard and jscpd adapters; adapter outputs are not dead code.
- [ ] One metrics evaluation does not rerun the same external analyzer once per registered metric.
- [ ] Missing lizard, scc, or jscpd yields an honest Unavailable reason naming the missing producer; no zeros and no raw exception string.
- [ ] Python and Go interface tests assert loc, complexity, and duplication values through the real metrics entry point when tools are available.
- [ ] Core-only imports remain dependency-free; lint, typecheck, focused metrics tests, and the full default suite pass.

## Dependencies
Depends on 0734 (registered LOC migration), 21fb (lizard adapter), and 3ba0 (jscpd adapter). The cross-language validation story a532 must depend on this integration slice.

## Likely files
- src/rebar/metrics/analyzer.py or a focused analyzer-composition module
- src/rebar/metrics/git_metrics.py / registry integration seam
- src/rebar/_commands/metrics.py context cache
- tests/unit/metrics/ and tests/interfaces/test_metrics_polyglot.py
data.title: Wire complexity and duplication analyzer outputs into code_health metrics
